commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `suppressInNonDirect` option to runner failure text resolution, applies it to rate-limit/overload failure copy, and adds Discord group/channel regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +89. Total +103 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection rather than a live run: current main builds rate-limit/overload copy as non-generic failure text without the `Agent failed before reply:` marker, so the existing non-direct suppression guard returns the text unchanged. The PR’s new tests encode the same Discord group/channel path.
commands.review_followup: The next step is maintainer review plus contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof; there is no narrow automation repair because the code diff has no blocking finding and proof must come from an actual setup.

target.title: Suppress transient runner failures in channels
target.body: Stop openclaw from spamming channels with API rate-limited errors. Every new message in the channel will trigger this response from openclaw and it is very spammy. Handle them similar to generic errors and don't surface them in groups or channels. ## Summary - keep existing channel behavior for classified non-transient runner failures - add an explicit non-direct suppression opt-in for transient rate-limit/overload failure copy - cover Discord group/channel behavior for non-transient errors, default rate-limit silence, and silentReply.group=disallow ## Root cause Group/channel failure suppression only applied to generic runner failures or text containing `Agent failed before reply:`. Rate-limit failures bypassed that path because they are classified earlier into polished user-facing copy by `buildRateLimitCooldownMessage` or `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`, and then passed as non-generic failure text. Since those messages do not contain the `Agent failed before reply:` marker, `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation` returned the rate-limit text unchanged and the dispatcher delivered it to public channels. This change preserves the existing guard for other classified failures, but lets rate-limit/overload paths explicitly opt into default group/channel silent-reply behavior. ## Testing - Not run, per local workflow instruction. - `git diff --check -- src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` - `oxfmt` not run successfully on `box`: the checkout still reports missing `@oxfmt/binding-linux-x64-gnu`.
